purpose: Pinned BFO 2.0 upper-anchor bridge for OBO root/intermediate classes whose is_a->BFO edge exists in the source ontology's OWL but was DROPPED by the local kot-obo-extractor (it kept only is_a edges whose target is inside the extracted subset). Loading this bridge RECOVERS source-asserted upper structure so is_a* closure reaches a BFO category anchor. Consumed by poc/scale/src/census.ts (--bridge on by default).
epistemic_status: LIT-BACKED per row: each mapping is the named ontology's own published BFO commitment (verifiable in its canonical OWL / OBO Foundry bridge file). The choice of BFO anchor is the ontology's assertion, not a KoT stipulation. Coarseness notes are stated where a root spans more than one fine BFO class.

bfo_anchor_categories.BFO_0000017: disposition (realizable entity; function is a realizable entity)

root_bridges.label: biological_process
root_bridges.provenance: GO OWL: biological_process is_a BFO:0000015 (process). go-bfo bridge.

root_bridges.label: molecular_function
root_bridges.provenance: GO OWL: molecular_function is_a BFO:0000034 (function) is_a BFO:0000017 (realizable entity). Anchored at realizable-entity → disposition category.

root_bridges.label: cellular_component
root_bridges.provenance: GO OWL: cellular_component is_a BFO:0000004 (independent continuant). COARSE: some components are BFO immaterial entities (e.g. membranes as sites); census maps the whole subtree to object.

root_bridges.label: sequence_feature
root_bridges.provenance: SO OWL: sequence features are generically dependent continuants (information artifacts). so-bfo bridge.

root_bridges.label: injury
root_bridges.rationale: MONDO's disease root already reaches BFO:disposition in-extraction; 'injury' is a sibling orphan in the local subset. Mapped to BFO:0000016 (disposition) for consistency with MONDO's disease treatment. STIPULATED (not LIT-BACKED) because MONDO models injury via a mix of process/disposition/quality and the local subset does not carry the disambiguating axiom.

root_bridges.label: information content entity
root_bridges.provenance: IAO OWL: information content entity is_a BFO:0000031 (generically dependent continuant). Bridges OGMS classes that dangle to IAO.

root_bridges.label: data item
root_bridges.provenance: IAO OWL: data item is_a information content entity is_a BFO:0000031.

root_bridges.label: planned process
root_bridges.provenance: OBI OWL: planned process is_a BFO:0000015 (process). Bridges OGMS classes that dangle to OBI.

root_bridges.label: process (OBI)
root_bridges.provenance: OBI OWL: OBI:0000278 subtype of planned process → BFO:0000015.

ontology_orphan_fallback.provenance: CHEBI OWL: chemical entity (CHEBI:24431) is_a BFO:0000040 (material entity). The 41 local CHEBI classes are a flat orphan subset (the CHEBI:24431 root is not in the local extraction); all are material entities → object.
ontology_orphan_fallback.applies_to: CHEBI classes with no is_a edge

ontology_orphan_fallback.rationale: A taxon (e.g. Mammalia) is a type whose instances are organisms = BFO:0000040 material entities → object; design large-kernel-scale-track.md §0 permits counting taxa as biological Kinds. STIPULATED because NCBITaxon does not itself assert a BFO parent (it is a pure taxonomy); the material-entity reading is the standard OBI/ENVO organism treatment, applied here as a policy.
ontology_orphan_fallback.applies_to: NCBITaxon classes with no is_a edge (the 402-record local subset is a flat orphan sample)
